question: What are the main functions of the pressurizer?

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates Reactor Coolant System (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0023.title: Reactor Coolant System High-Point Vents
nuscale_ch05_0023.summary: This section describes the testing and inspection requirements for the DHRS (Decay Heat Removal System) components in the NuScale reactor, ensuring compliance with ASME codes for Class 2 components. It details how actuation valves are exercised during cold shutdown conditions, outlines the necessary valve testing procedures, and specifies that an in-situ DHRS heat removal test will be conducted on the first installed reactor module as part of the initial test program.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and provides structural support and attachment points for various reactor components, with its materials and fabrication adhering to strict ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure long-term integrity.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation, eliminating the need for pumps or external piping, and is integrated within each NuScale Power Module. The RCS is designed to safely transfer heat from the reactor core, maintain proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and ensure fuel design limits are not exceeded during all operational conditions.

edges.description: Section A identifies the pressurizer as a component within the reactor vessel, while Section B provides specific details about the pressurizer's location, structure, and its principal function within the reactor vessel.

Section A identifies the pressurizer as a component of the reactor coolant system, while Section B provides a detailed description of the pressurizer's design and its principal function within that system.

Section A identifies the pressurizer as a component within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B specifies that the pressurizer is an integral part of the reactor coolant system, connecting their roles in the system's design.
